metadata.description: Threshold ADA Title I coverage, disability, and qualified individual determinations. All four components must resolve before downstream modules are invoked. Components 1.1 and 1.2 are rule_based (deterministic). Components 1.3 and 1.4 are standards_based (factor-weighted with yes/no/indeterminate output).

employer_coverage.description: Determines whether the employer is a covered entity subject to ADA Title I. Applies the 15-employee / 20-workweek threshold to private employers, and categorical coverage to governmental and organizational entities.

employer_employee_count.name: employer_employee_count
employer_employee_count.description: Total number of employees on the payroll during the relevant period. Required only for private_employer coverage threshold analysis. For other employer types, categorical coverage applies regardless of count.

workweeks_with_fifteen_plus.name: workweeks_with_fifteen_plus
workweeks_with_fifteen_plus.description: Number of calendar workweeks in the current or preceding calendar year during which the employer had 15 or more employees for each working day. Required only for private_employer threshold analysis.

employer_state.name: employer_state
employer_state.description: Two-letter state code where the employer operates. Reserved for future state-law overlay logic; not used in federal ADA coverage determination.

is_covered_employer.name: is_covered_employer
is_covered_employer.description: True if the employer is a covered entity under ADA Title I.

coverage_basis.name: coverage_basis
coverage_basis.description: Basis on which coverage is established or denied.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the coverage determination citing applicable source sections.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: List of source citations supporting the determination.

individual_status.description: Determines whether the individual is in a protected status under ADA Title I. Current employees and job applicants are covered. Former employees may assert limited post-employment claims (principally retaliation for protected activity). Independent contractors are generally not covered.

individual_status.name: individual_status
individual_status.description: Employment relationship status of the individual at the time of the alleged adverse action or accommodation request.

is_independent_contractor.name: is_independent_contractor
is_independent_contractor.description: True if the individual is classified as an independent contractor rather than an employee. Independent contractors are generally not covered by ADA Title I. This field triggers a bright-line exclusion when true.

employment_start_date.name: employment_start_date
employment_start_date.description: Date the individual began employment. Used for tenure context; not required for coverage determination.

employment_end_date.name: employment_end_date
employment_end_date.description: Date employment ended. Required when individual_status is former_employee; used to confirm the former-employee claim arose from the employment relationship.

claim_type.name: claim_type
claim_type.description: Nature of the claim being asserted by a former employee. Post-employment retaliation claims are covered; most other accommodation claims require current employment.

is_covered_individual.name: is_covered_individual
is_covered_individual.description: True if the individual is in a covered status under ADA Title I.

coverage_basis.name: coverage_basis
coverage_basis.description: Basis for individual status coverage or exclusion.

coverage_limitations.name: coverage_limitations
coverage_limitations.description: Any limitations or caveats on the coverage determination (e.g., former employee limited to retaliation claims).

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the individual status determination.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: List of source citations supporting the determination.

disability_determination.description: Determines whether the individual has a qualifying disability under the ADA as amended by the ADAAA (effective January 1, 2009). Evaluates all three definitional prongs: actual disability, record of disability, and regarded as. The ADAAA standard is broad; 'substantially limits' is not a demanding threshold and mitigating measures are ignored (except ordinary eyeglasses/contact lenses). Individuals qualifying only under the regarded_as prong are NOT entitled to reasonable accommodation.
disability_determination.adaaa_note: This analysis applies only to conduct on or after January 1, 2009. Pre-ADAAA conduct uses the original 1990 definition. See EEOC-NVTA-2011-1 Q&A #1.

disability_basis.name: disability_basis
disability_basis.description: The prong of the ADA disability definition under which the individual claims coverage.

impairment_type.name: impairment_type
impairment_type.description: Category of physical or mental impairment as defined in 29 CFR § 1630.2(h). Use canonical vocabulary term.

impairment_description.name: impairment_description
impairment_description.description: Optional freeform description of the specific impairment (e.g., 'Type 2 diabetes managed with insulin'). Used to populate reasoning output; does not affect logic.

major_life_activities_affected.name: major_life_activities_affected
major_life_activities_affected.description: One or more major life activities the impairment substantially limits (or historically limited). Must use canonical vocabulary terms. For major bodily functions, use 'major_bodily_functions' as the parent term. Empty array with actual_disability basis triggers indeterminate output.

limitation_severity.name: limitation_severity
limitation_severity.description: Severity of the impairment's effect on the identified major life activities, assessed without regard to mitigating measures.

is_episodic_or_in_remission.name: is_episodic_or_in_remission
is_episodic_or_in_remission.description: True if the impairment is episodic in nature (e.g., epilepsy, asthma, PTSD) or currently in remission (e.g., cancer). When true, would_substantially_limit_when_active must also be provided.

would_substantially_limit_when_active.name: would_substantially_limit_when_active
would_substantially_limit_when_active.description: True if the episodic or in-remission impairment would substantially limit a major life activity when active. Governs qualification under 29 CFR § 1630.2(j)(1)(vii).

mitigating_measures_used.name: mitigating_measures_used
mitigating_measures_used.description: List of mitigating measures the individual uses (e.g., medication, prosthetics, hearing aids, assistive technology). Per ADAAA, these are NOT considered when assessing substantial limitation, EXCEPT ordinary eyeglasses or contact lenses.
mitigating_measures_used.notes: Ordinary eyeglasses and contact lenses are the only mitigating measures that ARE considered. All others are excluded from the substantial limitation assessment per 42 U.S.C. § 12102(4)(E).

ordinary_eyeglasses_only.name: ordinary_eyeglasses_only
ordinary_eyeglasses_only.description: True if the ONLY mitigating measure used is ordinary eyeglasses or contact lenses (fully corrective lenses). When true, the ameliorative effect of those lenses IS considered in the limitation assessment. See 29 CFR § 1630.2(j)(1)(vi).

employer_perceived_impairment.name: employer_perceived_impairment
employer_perceived_impairment.description: True if the employer perceived, believed, or assumed the individual had a physical or mental impairment.

adverse_action_based_on_perception.name: adverse_action_based_on_perception
adverse_action_based_on_perception.description: True if the employer took an adverse employment action (or failed to take a positive action) based on that perception.

perceived_impairment_transitory_and_mino.name: perceived_impairment_transitory_and_minor
perceived_impairment_transitory_and_mino.description: True if the perceived impairment is (or would be) both transitory (lasting six months or less) AND minor. Both conditions must be met to invoke this exclusion. Determined objectively, not by the employer's subjective belief. See 29 CFR § 1630.15(f).

is_per_se_substantially_limiting.name: is_per_se_substantially_limiting
is_per_se_substantially_limiting.description: True if the impairment type is one that EEOC regulations identify as virtually always substantially limiting (e.g., deafness, blindness, cancer, epilepsy, HIV, diabetes, MS, major depression, PTSD, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia). See 29 CFR § 1630.2(j)(3)(iii). When true, simplifies the substantial limitation analysis.

disability_type.name: disability_type
disability_type.description: The established prong of disability, or none.

accommodation_rights_attach.name: accommodation_rights_attach
accommodation_rights_attach.description: True if the disability determination supports a reasonable accommodation obligation. False for regarded_as-only coverage, where no RA obligation exists. See 29 CFR § 1630.9(e).

factors_for.name: factors_for
factors_for.description: Factors supporting a finding of qualifying disability. Populated in alphabetical order.

factors_against.name: factors_against
factors_against.description: Factors against a finding of qualifying disability. Populated in alphabetical order.

key_uncertainties.name: key_uncertainties
key_uncertainties.description: Specific information gaps or unresolved questions that affect the determination. Required when has_qualifying_disability is indeterminate.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the disability determination citing specific regulatory provisions.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: List of source citations supporting the determination.

qualified_individual.description: Determines whether the individual is a 'qualified individual with a disability' — i.e., whether they satisfy the skill, experience, education, and other job-related requirements of the position AND can perform the essential functions of the position with or without reasonable accommodation. Essential function status is itself a multi-factor determination. This component does not determine which specific accommodation is appropriate (that is Module 3); it only determines whether the individual can perform essential functions given that some accommodation may be possible.

job_title.name: job_title
job_title.description: Title of the position held or sought.

written_job_description_exists.name: written_job_description_exists
written_job_description_exists.description: True if a written job description exists for the position.

job_description_predates_posting.name: job_description_predates_posting
job_description_predates_posting.description: True if the written job description was prepared before the position was advertised or posted. Pre-posting job descriptions carry greater evidentiary weight in establishing essential functions. See 29 CFR § 1630.2(n)(3)(ii).

essential_functions_listed.name: essential_functions_listed
essential_functions_listed.description: List of job functions asserted by the employer to be essential. Each entry is a brief description of the function (e.g., 'lift boxes up to 50 lbs', 'operate forklift', 'answer customer calls').

can_perform_essential_functions_without_.name: can_perform_essential_functions_without_accommodation
can_perform_essential_functions_without_.description: True if the individual can perform all essential functions listed without any accommodation.

can_perform_essential_functions_with_acc.name: can_perform_essential_functions_with_accommodation
can_perform_essential_functions_with_acc.description: True if the individual can perform all essential functions listed with some form of reasonable accommodation. Set to true even if the specific accommodation has not yet been identified, when there is reason to believe an effective accommodation may exist.

functions_employee_cannot_perform.name: functions_employee_cannot_perform
functions_employee_cannot_perform.description: Subset of essential_functions_listed that the individual cannot perform even with accommodation. If populated, these functions drive the qualified_individual analysis.

time_spent_on_each_function.name: time_spent_on_each_function
time_spent_on_each_function.description: Map of function description to approximate percentage of work time spent on that function. High percentage supports essential status. Key must match an entry in essential_functions_listed.

consequences_of_not_performing.name: consequences_of_not_performing
consequences_of_not_performing.description: Map of function description to description of operational consequence if the function is not performed. Severe operational consequence supports essential status.

example.operate forklift: warehouse operations cease without qualified forklift operator

past_incumbents_performed_function.name: past_incumbents_performed_function
past_incumbents_performed_function.description: True if past holders of this position consistently performed the contested function. Historical performance by incumbents supports essential status.

number_of_employees_who_can_perform.name: number_of_employees_who_can_perform
number_of_employees_who_can_perform.description: Number of other employees available to perform the contested function. Low count (e.g., 0 or 1) supports essential status; high count suggests the function may be marginal.

position_exists_to_perform_function.name: position_exists_to_perform_function
position_exists_to_perform_function.description: True if the position exists primarily or significantly to perform the contested function. Strong support for essential status.

meets_skill_education_experience_require.name: meets_skill_education_experience_requirements
meets_skill_education_experience_require.description: True if the individual satisfies the non-medical qualification requirements of the position (education, licensure, experience, skills). Failure here defeats qualified individual status independent of disability analysis.

accommodation_explored.name: accommodation_explored
accommodation_explored.description: True if a good-faith effort has been made to identify a reasonable accommodation that would enable performance of the function(s) in question. False triggers indeterminate output when functions_employee_cannot_perform is non-empty.

essential_functions_analysis.name: essential_functions_analysis
essential_functions_analysis.description: Analysis of essential function status and qualified individual determination.

functions_confirmed_essential.name: functions_confirmed_essential
functions_confirmed_essential.description: Functions with strong multi-factor support for essential status.

functions_possibly_marginal.name: functions_possibly_marginal
functions_possibly_marginal.description: Functions whose essential status is uncertain based on available inputs.

factors_for.name: factors_for
factors_for.description: Factors supporting qualified individual status. Alphabetical order.

factors_against.name: factors_against
factors_against.description: Factors against qualified individual status. Alphabetical order.

key_uncertainties.name: key_uncertainties
key_uncertainties.description: Specific information gaps affecting the determination. Required when is_qualified_individual is indeterminate.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the qualified individual determination citing applicable source sections.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: List of source citations supporting the determination.
